neighborhoods.name: Etna Nord (North Slope)
neighborhoods.address: Castiglione di Sicilia, 95012 Catania province, Sicily, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The north slope of Mount Etna, centred on the communes of Castiglione di Sicilia, Randazzo and Linguaglossa, is the most prestigious and sought-after sub-zone of the Etna DOC. High-altitude sites between 400 and 1,000 metres on ancient black basalt produce Nerello Mascalese reds of remarkable finesse, with individual contrade including Calderara Sottana, Guardiola, San Lorenzo, Feudo di Mezzo, Arcuria, Rampante and Santo Spirito increasingly bottled as single-vineyard wines.
neighborhoods.vibe: Terraced volcanic lava stone walls, ancient pre-phylloxera alberello vines, contrade names like a Burgundy premier cru, and the brooding presence of an active volcano on every label.

verified.address_quoted: Castiglione di Sicilia, Catania province, Etna DOC

neighborhoods.name: Etna Est - Milo (East Slope)
neighborhoods.description: Milo on Etna's east slope is the exclusive production zone for Etna Bianco Superiore, the DOC's prestige white designation. Only Carricante vineyards planted within the Milo comune qualify for the Superiore label, which requires higher minimum alcohol and lower yields. The east slope faces the Ionian Sea and enjoys a distinct maritime influence that gives Carricante its characteristic saline mineral freshness.
neighborhoods.vibe: Ancient Carricante vines facing the Ionian, alpine freshness meeting volcanic mineral force, the white counterpart to Etna Nord's red reputation.

neighborhoods.name: Vittoria (Cerasuolo DOCG)
neighborhoods.description: The Vittoria zone in the Ragusa, Catania and Caltanissetta provinces is the home of Sicily's only DOCG appellation: Cerasuolo di Vittoria, awarded in 2005. The blend requires minimum 50% Nero d'Avola and minimum 30% Frappato, producing a fresh, garnet red of medium body. The classico sub-zone requires higher percentages and longer ageing. COS, Arianna Occhipinti and Valle dell'Acate are the benchmark producers.
neighborhoods.vibe: The flat sun-baked calcareous plain of the Ragusa plateau; a natural wine heartland producing Sicily's most gastronomically versatile reds.

verified.address_quoted: Vittoria (RG), Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG zone, Sicily

neighborhoods.name: Marsala
neighborhoods.description: Marsala DOC, established in 1969 and centred on the city of Marsala in western Sicily, is the historic home of Sicily's most famous fortified wine. The Marsala production zone covers the entire Trapani province. Wine styles range from Fine (1 year ageing) through Superiore, Superiore Riserva to the prestige Vergine and Vergine Stravecchio Riserva (minimum 10 years ageing) produced from Grillo, Catarratto and Inzolia. Marco De Bartoli's artisan revival and the historic houses Florio and Pellegrino define the zone.
neighborhoods.vibe: Salt pans, shallow Stagnone lagoon, bleached tuff-stone cellars, and the bittersweet complexity of an ancient fortified wine tradition being rediscovered.

neighborhoods.name: Pantelleria
neighborhoods.classification: Passito di Pantelleria DOC, Pantelleria DOC
neighborhoods.description: Pantelleria is a volcanic island 70 kilometres from Tunisia, closer to Africa than to Sicily. Its Zibibbo (Moscato d'Alessandria) vines are trained in the alberello pantesco bush-vine system, a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage technique developed to withstand the fierce Scirocco and Tramontana winds. Passito di Pantelleria DOC from sun-dried Zibibbo grapes produces some of Italy's most opulent and complex sweet wines, led by Donnafugata Ben Rye, Marco De Bartoli Bukkuram and Serragghia.
neighborhoods.vibe: African heat, dammuso stone farmhouses, terraced black lava, and the intoxicating aroma of sun-dried Zibibbo on a windswept volcanic island.

neighborhoods.name: Noto (Nero d'Avola heartland)
neighborhoods.description: The Noto area in the south-east Syracuse province is widely regarded as the homeland of Nero d'Avola, Sicily's most important red variety. The Noto DOC covers the communes of Noto, Rosolini, Pachino and Avola. The combination of calcareous clay soils, extreme heat and low rainfall produces concentrated, ink-dark Nero d'Avola with particular depth. Gulfi, Zisola (Mazzei), Planeta Buonivini and Maggio Vini are the key producers. The Noto DOC also covers Moscato di Noto, a delicate sweet white.
neighborhoods.vibe: Baroque golden limestone towns, Ionian sea light, and some of Sicily's most powerful and sun-drenched Nero d'Avola vineyards.

neighborhoods.name: Menfi
neighborhoods.description: Menfi DOC on Sicily's south-west coast in the Agrigento province is the epicentre of the cooperative wine movement. Cantine Settesoli, with over 2,000 grower-members and 6,000 hectares, and Planeta's flagship Ulmo estate both anchor the zone. The sea breeze from the Mediterranean Canal keeps temperatures moderate, producing fresh, internationally styled Nero d'Avola, Syrah and Chardonnay. Menfi's wines are accessible and food-friendly, well suited to the region's seafood-rich cuisine.
neighborhoods.vibe: Sea-breeze vineyards stretching to the Mediterranean, cooperative viticulture on a grand scale, and Planeta's award-winning Ulmo estate as the quality benchmark.

neighborhoods.name: Aeolian Islands (Malvasia delle Lipari)
neighborhoods.description: The Aeolian Islands off the north-east coast of Sicily are home to Malvasia delle Lipari DOC, one of Italy's most distinctive island appellations. Produced primarily on Salina (the island with the most vineyards) from Malvasia di Lipari grapes - a small-berried, amber-skinned variety with intense floral and apricot character. Both the naturale (not dried) and passito (sun-dried) styles are made; the passito is more common and sought-after. Hauner (founded 1963) and Caravaglio are the benchmark producers.
neighborhoods.vibe: Volcanic island rising from an ultramarine sea, wild capers in bloom, and amber Malvasia passito with the scent of apricot, orange blossom and volcanic mineral.

verified.address_quoted: Santa Marina di Salina (ME), Aeolian Islands, Sicily

neighborhoods.name: Faro (Messina Straits)
neighborhoods.description: Faro DOC is one of Sicily's smallest and rarest appellations, covering the hills above Messina where the island nearly meets the Italian peninsula. The DOC requires Nerello Mascalese as the backbone with Nocera, Nerello Cappuccio, Calabrese and other blending varieties. Production volumes are tiny. The appellation has an ancient history - Faro wines were praised by ancient Roman writers - but saw near-extinction before a small revival in recent decades.
neighborhoods.vibe: Steep terraced hillsides above the Strait of Messina, ancient vines facing the Italian mainland, and a rare DOC being slowly reclaimed by dedicated small producers.

neighborhoods.name: Contessa Entellina
neighborhoods.address: Contessa Entellina, 90030 Palermo province, Sicily, Italy
neighborhoods.description: Contessa Entellina DOC in the central-western Palermo province is the historic home of Donnafugata, whose name and identity were inspired by this hilly landscape associated with the novel Il Gattopardo (The Leopard). The DOC covers a range of white and red varieties on the limestone-clay soils of the inland hills. Donnafugata's Contessa Entellina estate is the primary benchmark of the appellation.
neighborhoods.vibe: Rolling limestone hills of the Sicilian interior in the heart of Il Gattopardo country; Donnafugata's flagship estate defines the DOC for modern wine lovers.

neighborhoods.name: Alcamo
neighborhoods.description: Alcamo DOC in north-western Sicily around the city of Alcamo is one of the island's historic white wine zones, best known for Catarratto-based whites. The appellation also covers reds and ros from native varieties. Alcamo Bianco Classico from Catarratto has been produced since at least the 17th century. Rallo and smaller producers maintain the tradition of this gentle hilly appellation.
neighborhoods.vibe: Gentle rolling hills between Trapani and Palermo, ancient Catarratto vines, and the historic Alcamo Bianco Classico tradition of western Sicily.

neighborhoods.name: Mamertino (Milazzo)
neighborhoods.description: Mamertino DOC near Milazzo on Sicily's north-east Tyrrhenian coast is an ancient appellation praised by Julius Caesar who reportedly served it at his third consulship. The DOC requires blends based on Nero d'Avola with Nocera as the characteristic local blending component. Very few producers work within the Mamertino DOC today, making it one of Sicily's most historically significant but least-known appellations.
neighborhoods.vibe: Rocky Tyrrhenian coastline, the promontory of Milazzo, and ancient Roman wine history meeting one of Sicily's most obscure modern appellations.
